title: Science of Cirurgie
summary: The repository artifact is a copy or version of University of Michigan electronic item AHA2727, encoded from Robert von Fleischhacker's 1894 EETS edition. Fleischhacker prints the near-complete Middle English translation in Bodleian MS Ashmole 1396 and a second, partial copy in British Library Additional MS 12056 as parallel streams through printed p. 157, then continues Ashmole through p. 360. His edition is composite at selected points: bracketed Ashmole restorations use Additional 12056, while a lacuna on pp. 258-261 is supplied in Latin from the 1546 Venice Ars chirurgica anthology and Additional MS 26106. Complete scans of the 1894 edition, the 1546 anthology, and Wellcome MS 564 are cached; the latter preserves indirect selected reuse in a different 1392 surgical compilation and is not modeled as a direct complete third witness.
review_notes: The Middle English work, Lanfranc's Latin source, physical witnesses, 1546 Latin print, Fleischhacker edition, U-M encoding, repository artifact, scans, OCR, catalogs, and scholarly studies remain separate layers.
The Ashmole contents-list trailer on printed p. 4 is not a witness ending: the Ashmole main text runs from p. 7 through all five treatises, an epilogue, and the final table on p. 360.

Additional MS 12056 is a parallel direct witness through Treatise II chapter IV and also supplies selected bracketed restorations; its complete preliminary index does not prove that the surviving copy was once complete.

The complete 1546 BSB bundle contains 776 canvases at a documented 1200-pixel-width derivative profile. The cataloged Middle English work is absent; only a Latin source passage at fols. 239v-240v, canvases 494-496, is connected by a scoped edition relation.

Wellcome MS 564 is an indirect reception witness: excerpts from the same translation tradition are embedded non-contiguously in another compilation. No direct copied-from relation to Ashmole or Additional 12056 is asserted.
Current U-M No Copyright-United States language and the conditional 2006 availability notice embedded in the XML are both preserved rather than silently reconciled.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aha2727
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The pinned submodule source is not modified by these manifests.

entities.label: University of Michigan AHA2727 electronic text
entities.description: A structurally encoded electronic representation of Fleischhacker's 1894 edition, released through the Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse.
notes: The encoding retains Fleischhacker's two witness streams, notes, folio milestones, and printed pagination. It applies the edition's correction readings but does not encode the physical correction sheet as a separate leaf.

entities.label: Fleischhacker 1894 EETS edition of Science of Cirurgie

bibliographic.citation: Lanfranc of Milan, Lanfrank's ‘Science of Cirurgie,’ ed. Robert von Fleischhacker, Early English Text Society, Original Series 102 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1894).
bibliographic.title: Lanfrank's ‘Science of Cirurgie’
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 102
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society
bibliographic.pages: 360 pages, with preliminary title, correction sheet, and blanks

notes: The volume states ‘Part I—Text’ and ‘No more published.’

Pages 1-157 print Ashmole 1396 and Additional 12056 streams; p. 158 onward continues Ashmole only. Bracketed restorations and the pp. 258-261 Latin supply make the edition more than a purely diplomatic parallel transcription.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Ashmole 1396
entities.description: A fourteenth-century English parchment codex containing a Middle English translation of Lanfranc's Chirurgia magna and a following group of recipes. Fleischhacker's near-complete principal stream maps the translation through ff. 1r-272v.

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.notes: Fleischhacker's approximate 1380 date is retained as an edition-layer assertion, not substituted for the current broad catalog date.

holding.notes: The official TEI catalog description identifies the Lanfranc Middle English translation and a following recipe item.

physical_description.extent: Lanfranc translation transcribed by Fleischhacker through ff. 1r-272v; following recipes are outside the cataloged work
notes: Produced in England.
The current abbreviated online description derives from William Henry Black's 1845 Ashmole catalogue.

entities.label: British Library Additional MS 12056
entities.description: A fifteenth-century vellum-and-paper medical manuscript containing a Middle English version of Lanfranc's larger surgical work, imperfect at the end. Fleischhacker prints its contents list and surviving text at ff. 31r-86v, ending in Treatise II chapter IV.

holding.notes: The current catalog exposes no digitized-content link and offers physical-item request access.

physical_description.extent: Lanfranc component at ff. 31r-86v in Fleischhacker's transcription; imperfect at end

entities.label: British Library Additional MS 26106
entities.description: A fourteenth-century Latin medical codex, probably produced in southern France. Lanfranc's Chirurgia magna occupies the component beginning at f. 14; Fleischhacker says his Latin supply on pp. 258-261 also uses this manuscript beginning at f. 71.

holding.notes: The current catalog exposes no digitized content and requires physical-item request procedures, including a letter of introduction.

physical_description.extent: Lanfranc Chirurgia magna begins at f. 14 and is followed by Theodoric's Chirurgia at f. 110; Fleischhacker's cited passage begins at f. 71
notes: The manuscript was probably produced in southern France.
The exact endpoint and token-level contribution of its f. 71 passage to Fleischhacker pp. 258-261 remain unresolved.

entities.label: Ars chirurgica (Venice, 1546), Lanfranc Chirurgia magna component
entities.description: A 1546 Venice anthology of Latin surgical works printed by the Giunta press. The Lanfranc Chirurgia magna component begins at fol. 207r and ends at fol. 261r; Fleischhacker's supplied Latin begins at fol. 239v.

bibliographic.citation: Ars chirurgica, containing Lanfranc of Milan's Chirurgia magna (Venice: heirs of Lucantonio Giunta, 1546).
bibliographic.title: Ars chirurgica

holding.notes: The BSB digital object supplies a complete 776-canvas facsimile and page-level OCR.

entities.label: Wellcome Collection complete scan of Fleischhacker's 1894 edition
entities.description: A 376-image digital object rendered as a 377-page PDF, including provider cover, title matter, correction sheet, blanks, and printed pp. 1-360.
notes: The physical copy lacks its title page; the digital object supplies a xerox title page. The scan title visibly says approximately 1380 for Ashmole, while the current catalog description says approximately 1390.

entities.label: Internet Archive OCR for Wellcome item b29003416
entities.description: A complete OCR text derivative and scan-structure record for the Wellcome scan of Fleischhacker's 1894 edition.
entities.reproduction_format: Plain-text OCR with XML scan structure and JSON metadata

entities.label: BSB/MDZ complete digital facsimile of Ars chirurgica (1546)
entities.description: The complete 776-canvas IIIF Presentation object for BSB Res/2 Chir. 3, cached at a practical 1200-pixel-width derivative profile.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and Image API page images

entities.label: BSB hOCR for the 1546 Latin supply pages
entities.description: Provider hOCR for canvases 494-496, the verified fol. 239v-240v passage used by Fleischhacker.
notes: This is a searchable derivative of the exact supporting Latin passage, not an encoding of the cataloged Middle English work.

entities.label: Wellcome Collection MS 564
entities.description: A c. 1475 medical manuscript whose anonymous surgical compilation at ff. 10r-128r was completed in 1392 and embeds selected passages from the Science of Cirurgie translation tradition. It is an indirect, non-contiguous reuse rather than a complete direct witness.

date_statements.label: date stated by embedded surgical compilation

holding.notes: The current catalog provides a complete digital object and detailed component description.

physical_description.support: Paper, except the final vellum leaf
physical_description.extent: 201 leaves; the 1392 surgical compilation is at ff. 10r-128r
notes: The codex contains multiple medical works; only non-contiguous passages within the 1392 compilation reuse the target translation.

entities.label: Wellcome Collection complete digital facsimile of MS 564
entities.description: The complete 422-image Wellcome digital object rendered as a 423-page PDF, covering the entire 201-leaf codex and binding surfaces.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 3 manifest and complete page-image PDF

entities.label: Caparrini 2022 study of the Middle English Chirurgia magna
entities.description: A peer-reviewed specialist article on the Science of Cirurgie translation, witnesses, five-treatise structure, omissions, remedies, and Middle English-Latin bilingualism.

bibliographic.citation: Marialuisa Caparrini, ‘I rimedi medici nella versione in inglese medio della Chirurgia magna di Lanfranco da Milano: alcune considerazioni preliminari sui casi di bilinguismo latino-volgare,’ Medioevo Europeo 6.2 (2022), 21-44.

entities.label: Colafrancesco 2025 study of Wellcome MS 564
entities.description: A specialist article comparing the Science of Cirurgie tradition with the selected passages embedded in Wellcome MS 564 and assessing their indirect and possibly contaminated relationship.

bibliographic.citation: Sonia Colafrancesco, ‘Il manoscritto Wellcome 564: un testimone “trascurato” della traduzione medio inglese della Chirurgia magna di Lanfranco da Milano,’ Annali Online di Ferrara – Lettere 20 (2025), 327-359.

agents.name: Lanfranc of Milan
notes: Author of the Latin Chirurgia magna, not the anonymous English translator.

A request route is not evidence that the complete codex will be approved for scanning.

work_portion.label: Non-contiguous Science of Cirurgie-derived passages in the 1392 surgical compilation
locators: host compilation ff. 10r-128r = provider PDF pages 30-266 = IIIF canvases 29-265
identified target parallels: Pars I.i.2-7, I.iii.10, II.i.1-20, and II.ii.1, dispersed within the host compilation
verified example f. 12r = PDF page 34 = IIIF canvas 33: bone-regeneration passage from Science of Cirurgie I.iii.4
verified example f. 64r = PDF page 138 = IIIF canvas 137: modified remedy passage from Science of Cirurgie I.iii.3
verified example f. 74v = PDF page 159 = IIIF canvas 158: repeated bone-regeneration passage from Science of Cirurgie I.iii.4
verified example f. 78v and following = PDF page 167 / IIIF canvas 166 onward: adapted contusion passage from Science of Cirurgie I.iii.5
verified example f. 90v = PDF page 191 = IIIF canvas 190: Lanfranc haemorrhage exemplum from Science of Cirurgie I.iii.9
verified example ff. 96r-96v = PDF pages 202-203 = IIIF canvases 201-202: rewritten spasm exemplum based on Science of Cirurgie I.iii.15
verified example f. 107v = PDF page 225 = IIIF canvas 224: ulcer-remedy adaptation based on Science of Cirurgie I.iii.11
verified Lanfranc-attributed example f. 111v = PDF page 233 = IIIF canvas 232
notes: The complete codex is cached. These locators identify indirect selected reuse and do not imply that all ff. 10r-128r reproduce the target work.

Colafrancesco's comparison table identifies target sections but does not publish exhaustive manuscript start and end folios for every non-contiguous excerpt. The listed folio-to-PDF/canvas pairs are the exact examples located from her notes and directly checked in the complete facsimile; a full passage-boundary inventory remains unresolved.
PDF integrity and 423-page count were verified.

rights.component: The local U-M-derived XML transcription and markup
rights.basis: U-M currently labels the displayed item public domain in the United States, but the checked-in serialization retains a conditional 2006 notice and no direct evidence resolves rights in this repository artifact's transcription/markup layer.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M CME item AHA2727
rights.basis: The current item page states that the materials are in the public domain and applies No Copyright-United States.
notes: Whether the current statement supersedes the older conditional XML notice is unresolved.

rights.component: The 1894 edition, transcription, notes, apparatus, typography, and correction sheet
rights.basis: U-M's current item page applies No Copyright-United States to the material, and Internet Archive metadata marks the Wellcome-sourced 1894 volume with the Public Domain Mark.

rights.component: Physical manuscript as a material object and custody/access layer
rights.basis: Copyright status is not used here to characterize physical custody or access. No item-level digital reproduction license was found because no complete public facsimile was verified.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two direct Middle English witnesses of different completeness and dialect
editorial_practices.representation: Parallel Ashmole and Additional 12056 streams through printed p. 157, followed by Ashmole alone
editorial_practices.description: Fleischhacker preserves the two witness-specific streams rather than silently conflating them, while notes and brackets record selected comparison and supply.
editorial_practices.scope: Printed pp. 1-157 in parallel; Ashmole continuation pp. 158-360

editorial_practices.source_feature: Lacunae and defective readings in the Ashmole witness
editorial_practices.representation: Bracketed text supplied principally from Additional 12056, with source notes
editorial_practices.description: The Ashmole stream includes substantial bracketed restorations from the second English witness, so it is not a mechanically pure diplomatic transcription.
editorial_practices.scope: Selected passages across the Ashmole stream, including substantial supplies on pp. 7-8, 32, 35, 38, 49, 72, 110, 117, 119, 123-124, 133, 146, and 152

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three or four missing Ashmole leaves between ff. 177v and 178r
editorial_practices.representation: A Latin passage inserted from the 1546 Venice print and Additional MS 26106
editorial_practices.description: Fleischhacker replaces the missing Middle English stretch on pp. 258-261 with Latin rather than translating or silently omitting it. The 1546 passage is verified at fols. 239v-240v/canvases 494-496; the exact Additional 26106 allocation beginning at f. 71 is unresolved.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed correction sheet and corrected readings
editorial_practices.representation: Corrected readings incorporated into the encoded text; correction sheet not represented as a separate leaf
editorial_practices.description: The XML text reflects the corrections but does not preserve the scanned correction sheet's material placement and wording as its own encoded object.
editorial_practices.scope: Entire AHA2727 encoding, with comparison to Wellcome PDF page 12

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/aha2727.xml.
evidence.locator: HEADER; DIV1-DIV4; PB; MILESTONE; NOTE1; titles, source, availability, and encoding statements
evidence.summary: Supplies the exact repository artifact, U-M identifier and publication statement, 1894 source statement, two witness streams, five-treatise structure, printed and folio locators, editorial notes, and conditional availability wording.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, AHA2727.
evidence.locator: item information, table of contents, access, and rights statement
evidence.summary: Confirms the current public electronic item, title and contents, and No Copyright-United States statement.

evidence.citation: Lanfranc of Milan, Lanfrank's ‘Science of Cirurgie,’ ed. Robert von Fleischhacker, EETS Original Series 102 (London, 1894), complete Wellcome Collection scan.
evidence.summary: The complete scan verifies the title and imprint, correction sheet, parallel witness layout, exact witness transitions, bracketed supplies, pp. 258-261 Latin insertion, final epilogue, and chapter table.

evidence.citation: Direct structural and visual comparison of the pinned aha2727 XML with the complete Wellcome scan of Fleischhacker's 1894 edition.
evidence.locator: XML DIV1-DIV4/PB/MILESTONE/NOTE1 against PDF title, correction sheet, printed pp. 1-360, and especially pp. 4, 7, 157-158, 258-261, 359-360
evidence.summary: Establishes exact page and folio ranges, confirms that the Ashmole trailer on p. 4 ends only its contents list, maps the Add 12056 endpoint, identifies bracketed witness supplies, and confirms that XML incorporates corrections without encoding the correction sheet as a separate leaf.
notes: The sha256 is for the inspected repository XML; the paired scan checksum is recorded in evidence:fleischhacker-scan.

evidence.citation: Wellcome Collection, Lanfrank's Science of cirurgie, work uz3rjeuj, digital item b29003416.
evidence.locator: description, physical copy note, image count, download, and rights
evidence.summary: Describes the 1894 volume as 360 pages and 376 images, identifies Wellcome copy M29879 and its supplied xerox title page, exposes the complete PDF, and applies the Public Domain Mark.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata, OCR, and scandata for Wellcome item b29003416.
evidence.locator: metadata title, identifier, files, imagecount, rights; complete OCR; scandata leaf structure
evidence.summary: Identifies the Wellcome-derived Internet Archive item, complete OCR and scan derivatives, 378 scan leaves including excluded cards, and the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record 20711, Lanfrank's Science of cirurgie.
evidence.locator: uniform title, publication, extent, series, and note
evidence.summary: Confirms the uniform title Chirurgia magna. English (Middle English), 1894 publication, 360-page extent, EETS Original Series 102, and ‘No more published.’

evidence.citation: Bibliothèque nationale de France authority record for Lanfranc de Milan.
evidence.summary: Identifies Lanfranc of Milan as a physician and surgeon, approximately 1245-1306, associated first with Milan and then France.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Ashmole 1396; pinned TEI source at bodleian/medieval-mss commit 30eeba18af5a67e9585a2477b48a0847607228a3.
evidence.locator: msIdentifier, history/origin, physDesc, and msContents items 1-2
evidence.summary: The official catalog identifies the shelfmark and ARKs, a fourteenth-century English parchment codex, the Middle English Lanfranc Chirurgia translation, and following recipes. It exposes no verified complete public facsimile.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special collections mediated copying; Order special collections; Imaging Services order pages.
evidence.locator: eligibility, order methods, contacts, reading-room request system, prices, and limitations
evidence.summary: Current official guidance provides two routes when no public facsimile exists: reference-quality manuscript scans through mediated copying or Imaging Services, and registered in-person consultation through Bodleian Archives and Manuscripts Requests in the Weston Library. Requests require the shelfmark and folio range and remain subject to condition and restrictions.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 12056, record 040-002041763.
evidence.locator: scope and content, date, physical description, access, and digitized-content status
evidence.summary: Identifies the fifteenth-century vellum-and-paper quarto, describes a Middle English version of Lanfranc's larger surgical work as imperfect at the end, provides physical request access, and exposes no digitized content.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 26106, record 032-002030307.
evidence.locator: scope and content, component starts, date, origin, physical description, access, and digitized-content status
evidence.summary: Identifies the fourteenth-century vellum quarto, probably from southern France, locates Lanfranc's Chirurgia magna beginning at f. 14 and Theodoric at f. 110, and records physical access without public digitized content.

evidence.citation: Fleischhacker 1894, p. 258 and notes through p. 261, compared with the pinned XML and BSB 1546 scan.
evidence.locator: printed pp. 258-261; Ashmole ff. 177v-178r; 1546 fol. 239v and following; Additional MS 26106 f. 71 and explicit f. 71v note
evidence.summary: Fleischhacker explicitly says three or four Ashmole leaves are missing and inserts Latin from the 1546 Venice ‘Editio Argellata’ at fol. 239 and Additional MS 26106 at f. 71. The 1546 beginning and continuation are independently verified; exact allocation between the two sources remains unresolved.
notes: The sha256 is for the repository XML encoding of the statement and supplied passage; the complete edition and BSB image checksums are recorded separately.

evidence.citation: Bayerische Staatsbibliothek, Ars chirurgica (Venice, 1546), Res/2 Chir. 3, digital object bsb10196443, IIIF Presentation manifest.
evidence.locator: metadata, rights, 776 canvases, folio labels, and Lanfranc component canvases 429-537
evidence.summary: The official manifest identifies the 1546 Venice anthology, BSB shelfmark, NoC-NC statement, and all 776 canvases. Direct folio mapping places the Lanfranc Chirurgia magna component at fols. 207r-261r, canvases 429-537.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of BSB bsb10196443 canvases 429-537 and provider hOCR for canvases 494-496.
evidence.locator: Lanfranc component fols. 207r-261r/canvases 429-537; supplied passage fols. 239v-240v/canvases 494-496
evidence.summary: Folio labels, page images, and hOCR verify the component boundary and locate the exact Latin passage beginning at fol. 239v that Fleischhacker uses on pp. 258-261.
notes: The checksum is for canvas 494 hOCR; canvases 495 and 496 have separate checksums in their local-copy records.

evidence.citation: Wellcome Collection, Henri de Mondeville, Chirurgia, plus miscellaneous receipts, MS.564, work jscd8js6.
evidence.locator: reference number, production, physical description, component contents, images, access, and rights
evidence.summary: Identifies MS.564 as a c. 1475, 201-leaf medical codex, describes the 1392 surgical compilation at ff. 10r-128r, and exposes the complete 422-image digital object.

evidence.citation: Wellcome Collection IIIF Presentation manifest and complete PDF for MS 564, b19730925.
evidence.locator: complete 422-image manifest, 423-page PDF, item metadata, and rights
evidence.summary: The official digital object covers the complete codex, supplies page images for the 1392 compilation and its selected parallels, and applies the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark.

evidence.citation: Direct folio, PDF-page, and IIIF-canvas inspection of Wellcome MS 564 cross-checked against Colafrancesco's Table 1 and passage notes.
evidence.locator: host ff. 10r-128r / PDF pp. 30-266 / canvases 29-265; target-derived examples f. 12r/p. 34/canvas 33, f. 64r/p. 138/canvas 137, f. 74v/p. 159/canvas 158, f. 78v/p. 167/canvas 166 onward, f. 90v/p. 191/canvas 190, ff. 96r-96v/pp. 202-203/canvases 201-202, f. 107v/p. 225/canvas 224, and f. 111v/p. 233/canvas 232
evidence.summary: Direct inspection verifies that the complete provider PDF adds one cover page before the 422 IIIF canvases and maps the manuscript's modern foliation to the cited target-derived examples. Colafrancesco identifies the non-contiguous target sections but does not publish exhaustive start and end folios for every excerpt, so the record distinguishes exact examples from the still-unresolved full boundary inventory.

evidence.citation: Marialuisa Caparrini, ‘I rimedi medici nella versione in inglese medio della Chirurgia magna di Lanfranco da Milano: alcune considerazioni preliminari sui casi di bilinguismo latino-volgare,’ Medioevo Europeo 6.2 (2022), 21-44.
evidence.locator: pp. 21-44, especially witness descriptions, five-treatise outline, omissions, translation character, and bilingual terminology
evidence.summary: Current specialist study supports the anonymous late-fourteenth-century translation, witness boundaries and dates, Ashmole omissions, five-treatise organization, translator competence, adaptive fidelity, and Middle English-Latin bilingualism.

evidence.citation: Sonia Colafrancesco, ‘Il manoscritto Wellcome 564: un testimone “trascurato” della traduzione medio inglese della Chirurgia magna di Lanfranco da Milano,’ Annali Online di Ferrara – Lettere 20 (2025), 327-359.
evidence.locator: pp. 327-359, especially principal witnesses, dialects, selected passage table, and stemmatic discussion
evidence.summary: Supports treating Ashmole and Additional 12056 as two principal witnesses of one translation, distinguishes their Central and West Midlands language, and identifies Wellcome MS 564 as indirect selected reuse from a possibly contaminated branch rather than a complete direct third witness.

open_questions.question: Can Ashmole MS 1396 be dated more narrowly than the current catalog's fourteenth century, and is a complete public digital facsimile available?
next_steps: Request current codicological and reproduction information from the Bodleian, quoting MS Ashmole 1396 and both catalog ARKs.
Do not treat Fleischhacker's approximate 1380 date as a current catalog assertion.

open_questions.question: Was Additional MS 12056 originally a complete copy of the translation, and can a reproduction clarify its lost ending?
next_steps: Request reproduction or conservation information from the British Library for ff. 31r-86v.
Treat the complete contents list as evidence of intended structure, not proof of former physical completeness.

open_questions.question: Which exact tokens on Fleischhacker pp. 258-261 derive from the 1546 Venice print and which from Additional MS 26106?
next_steps: Obtain and inspect Additional MS 26106 f. 71 and following.
Collate it against the verified 1546 fols. 239v-240v and Fleischhacker's inserted text.

open_questions.question: What exemplar or contaminated branch supplied the Science of Cirurgie excerpts embedded in the 1392 compilation copied in Wellcome MS 564?
next_steps: Extend passage-level comparison across every identified excerpt in the complete facsimile.
Do not assert direct descent from Ashmole 1396 or Additional 12056 without new evidence.

open_questions.question: Does U-M's current No Copyright-United States statement supersede the conditional availability wording embedded in the 2006 XML?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections for written clarification before redistributing the U-M-derived transcription or markup.
Treat the medieval text, 1894 edition, modern scans, and U-M encoding as separate rights components.
